questionText:
Consider the following:
1. Global System of Trade Preferences
2. Asian-Pacific Economic Cooperation
3. Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development
4. Asian Development Bank
India is a member of how many of the above organisations?
explanation:
● The Global System of Trade Preferences among developing countries was established in 1989 as a framework for preferential tariff reductions and other measures of cooperation to stimulate trade between developing countries. The United Nations Conference on Trade and Development secretariat provides substantive and administrative support to the operation of the GSTP Agreement. India is its member. So, point 1 is correct.
● The Asia-Pacific Economic Cooperation (APEC) is a regional economic forum established in 1989 to leverage the growing interdependence of the Asia-Pacific. APEC's 21 members aim to create greater prosperity for the people of the region by promoting balanced, inclusive, sustainable, innovative and secure growth and by accelerating regional economic integration. India’s request for becoming a formal member of the group, first made in 1993 and then in 2007 is yet to be accepted. So, point 2 is not correct.
● The Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD) is an intergovernmental economic organisation with 36 member countries. It was founded in 1961 to stimulate economic progress and world trade. India is not a member of OECD but has been a key economic partner. So, point 3 is not correct.
● The Asian Development Bank was conceived as a financial institution that would be Asian in character and foster economic growth and cooperation in one of the poorest regions in the world. The institution was opened in 1966 at Manila (Philippines) with 31 initial members. It has 67 members—of which 48 are from within Asia and the Pacific and 19 outside. India is one of the founding members. So, point 4 is correct.
So, India is a member of only two of the above organisations.
Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.
subject: Economics

questionText:
With reference to the Special Safeguard Mechanism of the World Trade Organisation (WTO), consider the following statements:
1. It allows a developing country to raise tariffs to deal with an increase in agriculture imports.
2. Unlike the Special Agricultural Safeguard mechanism, it cannot be exercised by developed countries.
3. It was adopted at the end of the Uruguay Round in 1995.
How many of the above statements are correct?
explanation:
● The Special Safeguard Mechanism of the World Trade Organization (WTO) is a tool that allows developing countries to raise tariffs temporarily to deal with import surges or price falls of agricultural products. It could be triggered if the import surge or price fall is big enough, without any need to test injury or to negotiate. So, statement 1 is correct.
● At the Doha Ministerial Conference, the developing countries were given the concession to adopt a Special Safeguard Mechanism (SSM) besides the existing safeguards (like the Special Agricultural Safeguard or the SSG). The SSG was available to all countries- both developing and developed whereas the SSM is proposed only for developing countries. So, statement 2 is correct.
○ The special safeguards provisions for agriculture differ from normal safeguards. In agriculture, unlike with normal safeguards:
■ higher safeguards duties can be triggered automatically when import volumes rise above a certain level, or if prices fall below a certain level; and
■ it is not necessary to demonstrate that serious injury is being caused to the domestic industry.
○ The special agricultural safeguard can only be used on products that were tariffied — which amount to less than 20% of all agricultural products (as defined by “tariff lines”). But they cannot be used on imports within the tariff quotas, and they can only be used if the government reserved the right to do so in its schedule of commitments on agriculture. In practice, the special agricultural safeguard has been used in relatively few cases.
● SSM was not adopted after the Uruguay Round in 1995. It was only at the 2015 Nairobi Ministerial Conference, that the WTO members decided to negotiate the SSM. So, statement 3 is not correct.
So, only two of the above statements are correct.
Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.
subject: Economics

questionText: ‘‘Special Data Dissemination Standard’’ related to financial data transparency is established by which one of the following?
explanation:
● The Special Data Dissemination Standard (SDDS) was established in 1996 by the International Monetary Fund (IMF) to guide members that have, or might seek, access to international capital markets in providing their economic and financial data to the public. It is aimed at guiding members to enhance data transparency and help financial market participants to assess the economic statistics of individual countries.
● SDDS Establishment: The Special Data Dissemination Standard (SDDS) was indeed established in 1996 by the International Monetary Fund (IMF).
○ SDDS Purpose: Its purpose is to guide members in providing transparent and accessible economic and financial data. Benefits of the SDDS includes improved access to capital, lower borrowing costs, and increased investor confidence.
○ Government of India and SDDS: The Government of India did subscribe to the SDDS in 2003.
○ Standing Committee on Economic Statistics (SCES): The SCES was established in 2019 by the Government of India with a mandate to review and improve economic data quality and accessibility. The Standing Committee on Economic Statistics (SCES) was renamed the Standing Committee on Statistics (SCoS) in July 2023.
○ SCES Mandate: The provided list of datasets (PLFS, ASI, etc.) is a subset of the SCES' mandate, which encompasses various economic and social statistics.
Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.
subject: Economics

questionText:
With reference to the General Agreement on Trade in Services (GATS), consider the following statements:
1. It is the first multilateral agreement covering trade in services.
2. It applies to services supplied in the exercise of governmental authority.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
explanation:
● The General Agreement on Trade in Services (GATS) is the first multilateral agreement covering trade in services. It was negotiated during the last round of multilateral trade negotiations, called the Uruguay Round, and came into force in 1995. So, statement 1 is correct.
● The GATS distinguishes between four modes of supplying services: cross-border trade, consumption abroad, commercial presence, and presence of natural persons.
○ Cross-border supply is defined to cover services flowing from the territory of one member into the territory of another member.
○ Consumption abroad refers to situations where a service consumer moves into another member's territory to obtain a service;
○ Commercial presence implies that a service supplier of one member establishes a territorial presence in another member's territory to provide a service.
○ Presence of natural persons consists of persons of one member entering the territory of another member to supply a service.
● The GATS applies in principle to all service sectors, with two exceptions. It excludes “services supplied in the exercise of governmental authority”. These are services that are supplied neither on a commercial basis nor in competition with other suppliers. Cases in point are social security schemes and any other public service, such as health or education, that is provided at non-market conditions. Furthermore, the Annex on Air Transport Services exempts from coverage measures affecting air traffic rights and services directly related to the exercise of such rights. So, statement 2 is not correct.
● All World Trade Organization (WTO) members are at the same time members of the GATS and, to varying degrees, have assumed commitments in individual service sectors.
Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.
subject: Economics

questionText: The “Special and Differential Treatment”, recently seen in the news, is a:
options: a) low cost financing mechanism of International Financial Corporation (IFC) to fund renewable energy projects of private sector
b) trade preference program of USA to promote economic development by allowing duty free imports from beneficiary countries
c) line of credit extended by European Union to least developed countries facing economic crisis
d) set of World Trade Organisation provisions that grants special rights and concessions to developing countries
explanation:
● “Special and Differential Treatment” provisions under the World Trade Organization (WTO) framework that gives developing countries special rights with a view that these economies are comparatively in a lower level of development than the developed ones.
● Some of the benefits given to the developing countries under this include:
○ Developing countries get longer time periods for implementing Agreements and commitments.
○ Measures to increase trading opportunities for developing countries
○ Provisions requiring all WTO members to safeguard the trade interests of developing countries.
○ Support to help developing countries build the capacity to carry out WTO work, handle disputes, and implement technical standards.
○ Provisions related to least-developed country (LDC) Members.
Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.
subject: Economics

questionText:
With reference to the United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea (UNCLOS), consider the following statements:
1. It established rules governing all uses of the oceans and their resources.
2. It prohibits construction of any artificial islands in the Exclusive Economic Zone (EEZ).
3. As per its rules, the States have the right to explore mineral resources in the continental shelves.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
explanation:
● The United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea was adopted in 1982. It lays down a comprehensive regime of law and order in the world's oceans and seas establishing rules governing all uses of the oceans and their resources. It embodies in one instrument traditional rules for the uses of the oceans and at the same time introduces new legal concepts and regimes and addresses new concerns. The Convention also provides the framework for further development of specific areas of the law of the sea. So, statement 1 is correct.
● According to Article 60 of the United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea (UNCLOS), artificial islands can be built in the Exclusive Economic Zone (EEZ). While Article 60 permits construction, subsequent articles (80 and 87) clarify that artificial islands do not have the same legal status as natural islands. They cannot generate their own exclusive economic zone or territorial sea and cannot be used to restrict navigation rights.
Example: China has constructed artificial islands. So, statement 2 is not correct.
● According to Article 61 of the UNCLOS, the States have the right to conserve living resources.
● As per Article 81 of the UNCLOS, States have the right to explore mineral resources in the continental shelves. So, statement 3 is correct.
● According to Article 58 of the UNCLOS, states cannot restrict innocent passage and the freedom of navigation for non-coastal states in the EEZ.
Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.
subject: Economics

questionText: The ‘Ottawa group’, sometimes seen in the news, aims to:
options: a) Address challenges that are putting the multilateral trading system under stress
b) Promote a sustainable diving and snorkelling tourism industry
d) Reform the membership of United Nations Security Council (UNSC)
explanation:
● Canada is leading a group of 14 like-minded World Trade Organization (WTO) members, known as the Ottawa Group (Australia, Brazil, Canada, Chile, European Union, Japan, Kenya, South Korea, Mexico, New Zealand, Norway, Singapore, Switzerland and the United Kingdom). The aim of this group is to help WTO Members address specific challenges like trade dispute settlements, negotiations, and transparency that are putting the multilateral trading system under stress. It was established in 2018.
● The objective of this bottom-up process is to contribute ideas and analysis towards achieving meaningful, realistic and pragmatic reforms to the WTO over the short, medium and long term. In 2020, Ottawa Group has agreed on an initiative, calling on the WTO members to increase their cooperation and work toward enhanced global rules to facilitate trade in essential medical goods.
Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.
subject: Economics

questionText:
Consider the following statements:
1. The Digital Services Act (DSA) is a law by the United Nations to regulate large platforms such as YouTube to moderate user content.
2. It is a set of common rules on intermediaries’ obligations and accountability across a single market.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
explanation:
● The Digital Services Act (DSA) is a new law by the European Union (EU) to regulate the way intermediaries, especially large platforms such as Google, Facebook, and YouTube, function when it comes to moderating user content. So, statement 1 is not correct.
● The DSA is a set of common rules on intermediaries’ obligations and accountability across a single market. It will give better protection to users and to fundamental rights online, establish a powerful transparency and accountability framework for online platforms and provide a single, uniform framework across the EU. So, statement 2 is correct.
● It will be landmark legislation to force big Internet companies to act against disinformation and illegal and harmful content. Social media companies will have to add new procedures for faster removal of content deemed illegal or harmful. These requirements and their enforcement will be centrally supervised by the European Commission itself.
● Online platforms must ensure that users can easily identify advertisements and understand who presents or pays for the advertisement. They must not display personalised advertising directed towards minors or based on sensitive personal data.
Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.
subject: Economics

questionText:
With reference to the ‘G20 Summit’, consider the following statements:
1. The Sherpa track is led by G20 Finance Ministers and focuses on international financial stability.
2. The Engagement Group brings together representatives of civil society and the private sector, to discuss issues of common concern.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
explanation:
● The G20 is a forum for international cooperation on financial and economic issues, and is made up of 19 countries and 2 regional organisations (European Union and African Union). Within the G20, there are several different tracks and groups that work on specific issues, including the Sherpa Track, Finance Track, and Engagement Group.
● The Sherpa Track is responsible for coordinating and preparing the agenda for the G20 summit meetings, which take place annually. The Sherpa is typically a senior government official or an expert in public life who is appointed by the G20 country's leader. The Sherpa is responsible for communicating with other G20 members and coordinating the work of various ministers and officials in the lead-up to the summit.
○ The Finance Track of G20 is responsible for issues related to finance and economic policy, and it is led by the G20 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors. The Finance Track focuses on issues such as international financial stability, monetary policy coordination, and financial regulation. It also deals with global economic governance and economic management in the wake of the global financial crisis. So, statement 1 is not correct.
● The Engagement Group of G20 is an important track which brings together representatives of civil society, the private sector, and academia with G20 representatives to discuss and provide recommendations on issues of common concern. The engagement group focuses on enhancing cooperation between G20 and non-G20 stakeholders and in particular, seeks to involve representatives of the private sector and civil society in the G20 process. So, statement 2 is correct.
Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.
subject: Polity

questionText: Which one of the following is the primary objective of ‘Moscow Format’?
options: a) Peaceful resolutions to the Russia-Ukraine conflict
b) Promote cooperation among the Arctic States for addressing issues related to the region
c) Facilitate reconciliation process in Afghanistan to establish peace
d) Reduce the number of nuclear warheads to below Cold War limits
explanation:
● The Moscow Format is one of the several dialogue platforms on Afghanistan — which began before the Taliban takeover of Kabul. It consists of Russia, China, Pakistan, Iran, Kazakhstan, Tajikistan, Kyrgyzstan, Uzbekistan, Turkmenistan and India.
● The key objective of the Moscow format of consultations is to facilitate the national reconciliation process in Afghanistan to establish peace. It is often used as an alternative to formal peace talks, and can involve a range of stakeholders, including governments, international organisations, civil society groups, and other non-governmental organisations.
Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.
subject: Polity

questionText: The ‘Global Crisis Response Group’, which provides policy recommendations on global issues relating to food, energy and finance, was established by:
explanation:
● The United Nations Secretary-General had established a Global Crisis Response Group on Food, Energy and Finance in the UN Secretariat. The United Nations Conference on Trade and Development will co-lead the workstream on Finance and coordinate the work of all three workstreams.
● Global Crisis Response Group provides policy recommendations on food, energy and finance.
○ On Food: It urges all countries to keep markets open, resist unjustified and unnecessary export restrictions, and make reserves available to countries at risk of hunger and famine.
○ On Energy: The use of strategic stockpiles and additional reserves could help to ease the energy crisis in the short term. But the only medium- and long-term solution is to accelerate the deployment of renewable energy.
○ On Finance: It needs urgent action by the G20 and international financial institutions to increase liquidity and fiscal space so that governments can provide safety nets for the poorest and most vulnerable.
Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.
subject: Polity

questionText:
Consider the following statements with reference to Youth20 (Y20):
1. It is the official youth engagement group for the G20 (Group of 20).
2. It focuses on themes of climate change and disaster risk reduction.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
explanation:
● The Youth20 (Y20) is the official youth engagement group for the G20 (Group of 20). The first ever Y20 Summit is to be held in India on the sidelines of the G20 Summit. So, statement 1 is correct.
● It will focus on themes of the future of work; climate change and disaster risk reduction; peacebuilding and reconciliation; and youth in democracy. So, statement 2 is correct.
● It will focus on global youth leadership and partnership and provides a platform to the youth to express their vision and ideas on the G20 priorities.
Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.
subject: Polity

questionText:
Consider the following pairs:
Sl. No. International Agreement/Convention : Subject
1. Wassenaar Arrangement : Controlling arms export
2. New START Treaty : Combating money laundering
3. Singapore Declaration : Nuclear arms reduction
How many of the above pairs are correctly matched?
explanation:
● The Wassenaar Arrangement is a multilateral export control regime established in 1996 to promote transparency and greater responsibility in transfers of conventional arms and dual-use goods and technologies, thus preventing destabilising accumulations. India became its member in 2018. So, pair 1 is correctly matched.
● New START Treaty is a nuclear arms reduction treaty between the United States and the Russian Federation with the formal name of Measures for the Further Reduction and Limitation of Strategic Offensive Arms. It was signed in 2010 in Prague, and, after ratification, entered into force in 2011. It is expected to last until 5 February 2026, having been extended in 2021. So, pair 2 is not correctly matched.
● In 2022, the ‘Singapore Declaration’ was adopted at the 17th Asia and the Pacific Regional Meeting of the International Labour Organization (APRM of ILO). The declaration aims to ensure social dialogue to address challenges associated with the labour market and finding solutions for crises like the COVID-19 pandemic, natural disasters, and economic uncertainty. It provides a shared vision of 10-point priorities of national action of members of the ILO to deal with issues like dwindling wages, inflation and unemployment. So, pair 3 is not correctly matched.
So, only one of the above pairs is correctly matched.
Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.
subject: Polity

questionText:
Which of the following statements are correct with reference to the Civil Nuclear Cooperation of India?
1. India has signed an agreement for peaceful application of nuclear energy with Japan.
2. India in cooperation with Russia is establishing a Nuclear Power Plant in Bangladesh.
3. India has signed a Nuclear Research & Development Cooperation Agreement with Canada.
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
explanation:
Civil Nuclear Cooperation of India:
● Civil Nuclear cooperation agreements for peaceful application of nuclear energy were signed with Japan, Great Britain, Vietnam, and Bangladesh. So, statement 1 is correct.
○ So far Inter Governmental Agreement (IGA) for co-operation in peaceful uses of nuclear energy have been signed with the following eighteen (18) countries: Argentina, Australia, Bangladesh, Canada, Czech Republic, European Union, France, Japan, Kazakhstan, Mongolia, Namibia, Republic of Korea, Russia, Sri Lanka, United Kingdom, United States of America, Vietnam and Ghana.
● Tripartite MoU signed between Russia, Bangladesh and India for establishing a Nuclear Power Plant in Bangladesh. According to Rosatom, India, Bangladesh and Russia on Thursday signed an agreement to allow Indian firms in construction and installation works in the “non-critical” category for the Rooppur nuclear power plant project. So, statement 2 is correct.
● India has signed a Nuclear Research & Development Cooperation Agreement with Canada. So, statement 3 is correct.
Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.
subject: Polity

questionText:
Consider the following statements with reference to the Windsor Framework:
1. It is an agreement on facilitation of cross-border paperless trade in Asia and the Pacific brokered by the World Bank.
2. It calls for the introduction of a green lane and red lane system for goods that will stay in Northern Ireland and those that will go to the European Union (EU).
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
explanation:
● The ‘Windsor Framework’ is a proposed post-Brexit legal agreement between the European Union (EU) and the United Kingdom. So, statement 1 is not correct.
● It will replace the Northern Ireland Protocol, which has created problems in both economic and political spheres. The framework has two crucial aspects:
○ the introduction of a green lane and red lane system for goods that will stay in Northern Ireland and those that will go to the EU respectively; So, statement 2 is correct.
○ the ‘Stormont Brake’, which allows Northern Ireland lawmakers and London to veto any EU regulation they believe affects the region adversely.
Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.
subject: Polity

questionText:
With reference to the ‘Doha Political Declaration’, consider the following statements:
1. It focussed on putting the Least Developed Countries (LDCs) back on track to achieve the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).
2. It pledged an annual financing of $100 billion to LDCs to invest in climate change mitigation projects.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
explanation:
● The Fifth United Nations Conference on the Least Developed Countries (LDCs) concluded in 2023 with adoption of the ‘Doha Political Declaration’ by the world leaders. The declaration made by the head of the States is a key outcome of the second part of LDC5 conference held under the theme “From Potential to Prosperity” at Qatar. The declaration focuses on implementing the Doha Programme of Action (DpoA) — the 10-year plan to put the world’s 46 most vulnerable countries back on track to achieving the UN-mandated Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). So, statement 1 is correct.
● The Declaration also requested the Secretary-General of the United Nations to ensure the full mobilisation and coordination of all parts of the United Nations system to facilitate coordinated implementation of the Doha Programme of Action. The Secretary-General has called for reform of the international financial architecture, coupled with an increase in development financing of at least $500 billion annually. However, the amount was not pledged in the Declaration. So, statement 2 is not correct.
Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.
subject: Environment

questionText: Which one of the following statements is correct with reference to the ‘Principle of Non-Refoulement’?
options: a) It is an agreement between countries to not use political influences to interfere in their respective internal matters.
b) It is the use of non-military tools by countries to disrupt the governance of enemy States.
c) It prevents the deportation of illegal immigrants by the States who face risk of persecution in their home country.
d) It is a ‘No First Use (NFU)’ policy that implies that nuclear weapons would only be used in response to a nuclear attack.
explanation:
● ‘Principle of Non-Refoulement’ is an essential protection under international human rights, refugee, humanitarian and customary laws, which prohibits States from deporting/removing illegal migrants from their jurisdiction or effective control when there are substantial grounds for believing that the person/group of people (illegal migrants) would be at risk of irreparable harm, including persecution, torture, ill-treatment, or other serious human rights violations upon their return to the native country.
● It is obligatory for India to follow the provisions under non-refoulement as Customary International Law. Though India is not a party to the UN Refugee Conventions, it is a party to the Universal Declaration of Human Rights of 1948 and the International Covenant on Civil and Political Rights of 1966. Article 21 of the Constitution of India encompasses the right of non-refoulement.
● The Supreme Court in the National Human Rights Commission vs. State of Arunachal Pradesh (1996) held that “while all rights are available to citizens, persons including foreign citizens are entitled to the right to equality and the right to life, among others”.
Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.
subject: Polity

questionText:
Consider the following:
1. United States of America
2. New Zealand
3. Japan
4. India
5. Singapore
How many of the above countries are part of the Comprehensive and Progressive Agreement for Trans-Pacific Partnership (CPTPP)?
explanation:
● The Comprehensive and Progressive Agreement for Trans-Pacific Partnership (CPTPP) is a free trade pact based around the Pacific rim. CPTPP is a free trade agreement (FTA) that was agreed in 2018 between 11 countries – Australia, Brunei, Canada, Chile, Japan, Malaysia, Mexico, New Zealand, Peru, Singapore and Vietnam. It does not have a single market for goods or services, and so regulatory harmonisation is not required, unlike the European Union. So, points 2, 3 and 5 are correct.
● Recently, Britain has decided to join CPTPP and will become the 12th member, and the first to join the partnership since its inception. The United States of America and India are not the members of CPTPP. So, points 1 and 4 are not correct.
So, only three of the above countries are part of the Comprehensive and Progressive Agreement for Trans-Pacific Partnership (CPTPP).
Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.
subject: Polity

questionText: The ‘Initiative on Critical and Emerging Technologies’ (iCET) is an agreement between India and which one of the following countries?
explanation:
● The ‘Initiative on Critical and Emerging Technologies’ (iCET) is a partnership between India and the United States of America (USA) to work together in developing important and new technologies. It involves collaboration in a range of areas including quantum computing, semiconductors, 5G and 6G wireless infrastructure, and civilian space projects such as lunar exploration.
● The iCET’s goal is to increase the technology interaction between the USA and India while also potentially adding additional strategic depth and breadth to their growing partnership. The Prime Minister’s Office in Delhi and the White House in Washington will oversee and direct the iCET.
Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.
subject: Polity

questionText:
Consider the following statements with reference to the Inter-Parliamentary Union (IPU):
1. It is an organ of the United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization (UNESCO) and is primarily headquartered in Paris.
2. It is financed primarily by its members out of public funds.
3. Its membership can be suspended in cases of unconstitutional dissolution of Parliament.
How many of the above statements are correct?
explanation:
● The Inter-Parliamentary Union (IPU) is the international organisation of Parliaments (Article 1 of the Statutes of the Inter-Parliamentary Union). It was established in 1889. The IPU is financed primarily by its members out of public funds. The site of the Union's Headquarters is Geneva (Switzerland). It supports the efforts of the UN, whose objectives it shares, and works in close co-operation with it. So, statement 1 is not correct and statement 2 is correct.
● All parliaments are eligible to join IPU if they are lawfully established national bodies operating in States or aspirational States recognized by the United Nations. On rare occasions, Members are suspended due to the unconstitutional dissolution of Parliament, or their rights are suspended for the non-payment of assessed contributions. So, statement 3 is correct.
● Decisions on admitting, readmitting and suspending Members or their rights are taken by the IPU Governing Council. The 179 Member Parliaments out of the 193 countries in the world are members of IPU. Both China and India are members of the IPU.
So, only two of the above statements are correct.
Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.
subject: Polity

questionText:
Consider the following statements with reference to the ‘Helsinki Rules, 1966’:
1. They were adopted under the aegis of the International Law Association.
2. These rules regulate the equitable utilisation of the waters of an international drainage basin.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
explanation:
● ‘Helsinki Rules’ was adopted by the International Law Association at the 52nd conference, held in Helsinki in 1966. Article 1 of these rules declares that these rules are the general rules of international law, applicable to the use of the waters of an international drainage basin except as may be provided otherwise by convention, agreement, or binding custom among the basin States. So, statement 1 is correct.
● These rules regulate the equitable utilisation of the waters of an international drainage basin, prevention of water pollution, navigation, timber floating, and procedures for the prevention and settlement of disputes. So, statement 2 is correct.
○ A drainage basin is an area of land where water from rain or snowmelt drains downhill into a body of water such as a river, lake, wetland, or ocean. The drainage basin includes both the streams and rivers that convey the water as well as the land surface from which water drains into those channels.
○ The boundary line separating one drainage basin from the other is known as the watershed which is also a small catchment area of small rivulets and rills originating at the end of the drainage basin.
Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.
subject: Polity

questionText:
With reference to the membership of the United Nations Human Rights Council (UNHRC), consider the following statements:
1. Its members are elected by the United Nations (UN) General Assembly through a direct and secret vote.
2. Its members serve for a period of five years.
3. Its members are not eligible for immediate re-election after serving two consecutive terms.
How many of the above statements are correct?
explanation:
● The United Nations Human Rights Council (UNHRC) is an inter-governmental body within the United Nations system. It is responsible for strengthening the promotion and protection of human rights around the globe and for addressing situations of human rights violations and making recommendations on them.
○ It replaced the former United Nations Commission on Human Rights, which operated from 1946 to 2006.
● The UNHRC is made up of 47 UN Member States, which are elected by the UN General Assembly through a direct and secret ballot. So, statement 1 is correct.
● Members of the Council serve for a period of three years and are not eligible for immediate re-election after serving two consecutive terms. So, statement 2 is not correct and statement 3 is correct.
● India is a member of UNHRC and its term expires in 2024.Almost two thirds of the UN Human Rights Council’s income come from voluntary contributions from Member States and other donors. The remainder is covered by the UN regular budget.
So, only two of the above statements are correct.
Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.
subject: Polity

questionText:
Consider the following:
1. Multidimensional Integrated Stabilization Mission in Mali (MINUSMA)
2. Mission for the Referendum in Western Sahara (MINURSO)
3. Military Observer Group in India and Pakistan
4. Interim Security Force for Abyei
How many of the above are United Nations peacekeeping missions?
explanation:
● United Nations Multidimensional Integrated Stabilization Mission in Mali (UN-MINUSMA) is a United Nations peacekeeping mission in Mali since 2013. Mali, a landlocked country and a cultural hub of West Africa, was overrun in 2012 by a coalition of Tuareg and terrorist groups which moved south toward the capital city of Bamako. So, point 1 is correct.
● The UN Mission for the Referendum in Western Sahara (UN-MINURSO) is in Western Sahara, a territory on the northwest coast of Africa bordered by Morocco, Mauritania, and Algeria, that was under Spanish control until 1976. Both Morocco and Mauritania affirmed their claim to the territory, which was opposed by the Frente Popular para la Liberación de Saguia el-Hamra y de Río de Oro (Frente POLISARIO) and fighting ensued which led to the establishment of MINURSO in 1991, in accordance with the settlement plan for a transitional period for the preparation of a referendum, in which the people of Western Sahara would choose between independence and integration with Morocco. So, point 2 is correct.
● United Nations Military Observer Group in India and Pakistan (UN-MOGIP) is an expanded UN mission that has supervised Jammu and Kashmir since 1949. The region of Kashmir, situated on the borders of India and Pakistan, has been disputed by the two countries since they became independent in 1947. Kashmir acceded to India, leading to an outbreak of violence that was ended by a ceasefire and the deployment of a UN peacekeeping force. So, point 3 is correct.
● The United Nations Interim Security Force for Abyei (UN-ISFA) is a United Nations peacekeeping mission in Abyei. After the civil war in Sudan, the 2011 referendum resulted in the independence of South Sudan. The Abyei area, which lies along the border, remains disputed land between Sudan and South Sudan. The primary objective of UNISFA is to monitor the border between north and south, facilitate the delivery of humanitarian aid and protect civilians and humanitarian workers in Abyei. So, point 4 is correct.
So, all four of the above are United Nations peacekeeping missions.
Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.
subject: Polity

questionText: The Washington Declaration, sometimes seen in the news, is related to:
options: a) Joint nuclear deterrence strategy of USA and South Korea
b) Reduction in nitrogen wastes by 50% by 2030
c) Developing Indian Ocean Region as a drug free zone
explanation:
● ‘Washington Declaration’ is an agreement between the USA and South Korea, which outlines a joint nuclear deterrence strategy. The declaration reaffirms that South Korea would not make its own nuclear capabilities and would instead focus on deterrence measures through an alliance-based approach.
● According to this declaration, an American nuclear ballistic submarine would be deployed in the Korean peninsula; a Nuclear Consultative Group would be formed to formulate principles of joint response tactics; South Korea would receive Intel from the U.S. regarding nuclear advancements; and the U.S. will strengthen South Korea’s nuclear deterrence capabilities through joint military training programs and an annual intergovernmental simulation.
Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.
subject: Polity

questionText:
Consider the following pairs:
Sl. No. Subsidy box : Description
1. Amber box : Subsidies allowed without limitations
2. Green box : Trade distorting subsidies
3. Blue box : Direct payments made to farmers by the government in form of assistance programmes
4. Development box : Investment subsidies available to agriculture
How many of the pairs given above are correctly matched?
explanation:
The subsidies provided by the government to the agricultural sector are termed by the World Trade Organization (WTO) as Aggregate Measure of Support (AMS). It is calculated in terms of product and input subsidies.
All subsidies which are supposed to distort production and trade fall into the Amber Box. Under the WTO provisions, these subsidies are subject to reduction commitment to their minimum level—to 5 percent and 10 percent for the developed and the developing countries of their total value of agricultural outputs per annum accordingly. So, pair 1 is not correctly matched.
The agricultural subsidies which cause minimal or no distortions to trade are put under the Green Box. They have to be government-funded (not by charging consumers higher prices) and must not involve price support.
The green box subsidies are allowed without limits provided they comply with the policy-specific criteria. It means this box is exempt from the calculation under subsidies under the WTO provisions because the subsidies under it are not meant to promote production thus do not distort trade. So, pair 2 is not correctly matched.
Blue Box is the amber box with conditions. Direct payments under production limiting programmes (often referred to as “Blue Box” measures) are exempt from commitments if such payments are made on fixed areas and yield or a fixed number of livestock. These subsidies are nothing but certain direct payments (i.e., direct set-aside payments) made to farmers by the government in the form of assistance programmes to encourage agriculture, rural development, etc. At present, there are no limits on spending on subsidies in the blue box. So, pair 3 is correctly matched.
Social and Development Box (S & D Box), There are also exemptions for developing countries (sometimes called an “S&D box” or "development box", including provisions in Article 6.2 of the Agreement) which allows the developing countries for some subsidies to the agriculture sector under certain conditions. They include investment subsidies which are generally available to agriculture in developing country members. These conditions revolve around human development issues such as poverty, minimum social welfare, health support, etc., especially for the segment of population living below the poverty line. Developing countries stressed the need to keep this, and perhaps add additional flexibilities. Particularly vocal are countries using the provision to diversify production away from illicit crops. So, pair 4 is correctly matched.
So, only two of the above pairs are correctly matched.
Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.
subject: Economics

questionText:
With reference to the International Monetary Fund (IMF), consider the following statements:
1. It lends to its member countries facing balance of payments problems in order to restore their economic growth.
2. All IMF members are eligible to access the Fund’s resources in the General Resources Account (GRA) on non-concessional terms.
3. It lends for specific developmental projects in underdeveloped countries.
How many of the above statements are correct?
explanation:
● The International Monetary Fund (IMF) was established in 1944 in the aftermath of the Great Depression of the 1930s. It aimed to ensure exchange rate stability and encourage its member countries to eliminate exchange restrictions that hindered trade.
● The IMF lends to its member countries facing balance of payments problems in order to facilitate the adjustment process and restore member countries' economic growth and stability through various loan instruments or "facilities". So, statement 1 is correct.
● All IMF members are eligible to access the Fund’s resources in the General Resources Account (GRA) on non-concessional terms, but the IMF also provides concessional financial support at zero interest rates through the Poverty Reduction and Growth (PRGT), which is better tailored to the diversity and needs of low-income countries. For emerging and advanced market economies in crises, the bulk of IMF assistance has been provided through Stand-By Arrangements (SBAs) to address the short-term or potential balance of payments problems. The Standby Credit Facility (SCF) serves a similar purpose for low-income countries. So, statement 2 is correct.
● The IMF provides financial support for balance of payments needs upon request by its member countries. Unlike development banks, the IMF does not lend for specific projects. Following such a request, an IMF staff team holds discussions with the government to assess the economic and financial situation, and the size of the country’s overall financing needs, and agree on the appropriate policy response. So, statement 3 is not correct.
So, only two of the above statements are correct.
Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.
subject: Economics

questionText:
With reference to the Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD), consider the following statements:
1. It was established to administer the Marshall Plan for post-Second World War reconstruction.
2. It has observer status at the United Nations.
3. It has developed Crypto Asset Reporting Framework to facilitate exchange of tax information on crypto assets.
How many of the above statements are correct?
explanation:
● Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD) formally known as Organisation for European Economic Co-operation (OEEC) was established to administer the predominantly U.S.-funded Marshall Plan for post- Second World War reconstruction of the European continent. So, statement 1 is correct.
● The mandate of the OECD is to promote policies that will improve the economic and social well-being of people around the world. Its headquarter is located in Paris in France. It has been granted an observer status at the United Nations General Assembly. So, statement 2 is correct.
● Australia, Austria, Belgium, Canada, Chile, Czech Republic, Denmark, Estonia, Finland, France, Germany, Greece, Hungary, Iceland, Ireland, Israel, Italy, Japan, Korea, Latvia, Luxembourg, Mexico, Netherlands, New Zealand, Norway, Poland, Portugal, Slovak Republic, Slovenia, Spain, Sweden, Switzerland, Turkey, United Kingdom, United States are member countries. India is not among the member countries of OECD.
● OECD has developed the Crypto Asset Reporting Framework (CARF). The OECD defines crypto assets as a digital representation of monetary value that relies on cryptographically secured technology. The framework provides for the automatic exchange of tax-relevant information on Crypto-Assets. It aims to increase transparency among nations. So, statement 3 is correct.
So, all three of the above statements are correct.
Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.
subject: Economics

questionText:
Consider the following:
1. Pakistan
2. Turkmenistan
3. Kyrgyzstan
4. Russia
5. Iran
How many of the above countries are the members of the Shanghai Cooperation Organisation (SCO)?
explanation:
● ‘Shanghai Cooperation Organisation (SCO)’ is a permanent intergovernmental international organisation created in Shanghai in 2001 by six member countries. Its main goals are strengthening mutual confidence and good-neighbour relations among the member countries, promoting effective cooperation in various aspects, making joint efforts to maintain and ensure peace, security, and stability in the region, moving towards the establishment of a new, democratic, just and rational political and economic international order.
● The grouping had six members (China, Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, Russia, Tajikistan, and Uzbekistan). India along with Pakistan joined the group in 2017. Iran is the newest member to join SCO in 2023.
Four countries namely Afghanistan, Belarus and Mongolia have observer status with the SCO, and six countries namely Azerbaijan, Armenia, Cambodia, Nepal, Turkey, and Sri Lanka have a dialogue partner status. So, points 1, 3, 4 and 5 are correct.
○ Turkmenistan is not a member of SCO. So, point 2 is not correct.
So, only four of the above countries are the members of the Shanghai Cooperation Organisation (SCO).
Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.
subject: Polity

questionText: Which one of the following statements is not correct with reference to the International Court of Justice (ICJ)?
options: a) It is the principal judicial organ of the United Nations (UN).
b) Only member States are eligible to appear before ICJ in contentious cases.
c) It has authority to try individuals accused of war crimes or crimes against humanity.
d) Its judgements in contentious cases are final and binding on the parties and are without appeal.
explanation:
● The International Court of Justice (ICJ) is the principal judicial organ of the United Nations (UN).
● The ICJ is not a Supreme Court to which National Courts can turn, it does not act as a court of last resort for individuals. It is an appeal court for any international tribunal. It can, however, rule on the validity of arbitral awards.
● ICJ has no jurisdiction to try individuals accused of war crimes or crimes against humanity. As ICJ is not a criminal court, it does not have a prosecutor able to initiate proceedings. Hence, option (c) is incorrect.
● Only member States are eligible to appear before ICJ in contentious cases. At present, this essentially means the 193 Member States of the United Nations. The Court has no jurisdiction to deal with applications from individuals, non-governmental organisations, corporations or any other private entity. It cannot provide them with legal advice or help them in their dealings with national authorities.
● The court's judgments in contentious cases are final and binding on the parties to a case, and without appeal.
Therefore, option (c) is the answer.
Knowledge Box
International Court of Justice (ICJ) and International Criminal Court (ICC):
● ICC was established by the Rome Statute. This treaty was negotiated within the UN, however, it created an independent judicial body distinct from the UN.
● The ICJ hears disputes between States whereas the ICC handles prosecutions of individuals.
● The ICC investigates and tries individuals charged with the gravest crimes of concern to the international community namely genocide, war crimes, crimes against humanity and the crime of aggression.
○ The ICC was established as a court of last resort to prosecute the most heinous offences in cases where national courts fail to act.
● Both ICJ and ICC are headquartered in the Netherlands at The Hague.
subject: Polity

questionText:
With reference to Trade-Related Aspects of Intellectual Property Rights (TRIPS), consider the following statements:
1. It allows members to exclude plant and animal inventions from patenting in their countries.
2. It allows compulsory licensing and government use of a patent without the authorization of its owner.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
explanation:
● The Agreement on Trade-Related Aspects of Intellectual Property Rights (TRIPS) is an international legal agreement between all the member nations of the World Trade Organization (WTO). TRIPS establishes minimum standards for the availability, scope, and use of seven forms of intellectual property: copyrights, trademarks, geographical indications, industrial designs, patents, layout designs for integrated circuits, and undisclosed information (such as trade secrets and test data).
● Article 27.3(b) of TRIPS agreement allows members to exclude some types of plant and animal inventions from patenting in their countries.
○ More specifically, it allows them to exclude from patentability “plants and animals other than micro-organisms”.
○ However, the agreement says members must provide for the protection of plant varieties, either by patents or by an effective sui generis system (i.e., a system created especially for this purpose) or by any combination of the two. So, statement 1 is correct.
● TRIPS allows compulsory licensing and government use of a patent without the authorization of its owner. But this can only be done under a number of conditions aimed at protecting the legitimate interests of the right holder. So, statement 2 is correct.
Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.
Knowledge Box
Agreement on Trade-Related Aspects of Intellectual Property Rights (TRIPS)
● TRIPS was negotiated at the end of the Uruguay Round of the General Agreement on Tariffs and Trade (GATT) between 1989 and 1990 and is administered by the WTO.
● It came into force in 1995, as part of the Agreement Establishing the World Trade Organization (WTO).
● It applies basic international trade principles to member states regarding intellectual property, including national treatment and most-favoured-nation treatment.
subject: Economics

questionText:
With reference to the World Bank, consider the following statements:
1. It was established as a result of the United Nations Monetary and Financial Conference of 1944.
2. It provides bailouts to countries facing economic crisis only on sovereign guarantees.
3. The United States of America is the largest shareholder of the World Bank, followed by China.
How many of the above statements are correct?
explanation:
● On July 1, 1944, during the Second World War, delegates from 44 nations met at Bretton Woods to participate in what became known as the Bretton Woods Conference, officially known as the United Nations Monetary and Financial Conference. It resulted in the establishment of the International Monetary Fund (IMF) and the International Bank for Reconstruction and Development (IBRD; later known as the World Bank) in 1944. So, statement 1 is correct.
● The World Bank does not provide bailouts for countries facing economic crises. Bailouts are provided by the International Monetary Fund in return for implementing specific conditions designed to put government finances on a sustainable footing and restore growth. So, statement 2 is not correct.
● The United States of America is the largest single shareholder, followed by Japan, Germany, the United Kingdom, and France. The rest of the shares are divided among the other member countries. A Board of Governors represents the Bank's government shareholders. So, statement 3 is not correct.
So, only one of the above statements is correct.
Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.
Knowledge Box
World Bank Group:
● Goal/Mission: ‘Working for a World Free of Poverty’
● Purpose: Bridge the economic divide between rich and poor countries.
● Headquarters: All the five organisations make up the World Bank Group and are headquartered at Washington D.C, USA.
● Decision making process: It runs like a giant cooperative, where its members are shareholders and is operated for the benefit of those using its services.
● Organisations which form part of it:
○ International Bank for Reconstruction and Development: lends to low- and middle-income countries;
○ International Development Association (IDA): lends to low-income countries;
○ International Finance Corporation (IFC): lends to the private sector;
○ Multilateral Investment Guarantee Agency (MIGA): encourages private companies to invest in foreign countries; and
○ International Centre for Settlement of Investment Disputes (ICSID): It helps private investors and foreign countries work out differences when they don't agree.
subject: Economics

questionText:
Consider the following pairs:
Sl. No. Report : Published by
1. Poverty and Shared Prosperity Report : World Economic Forum
2. Global Hunger Index : United Nations Development Programme (UNDP)
3. Global Education Monitoring Report : UNESCO
How many of the above pairs are correctly matched?
explanation:
● Poverty and Shared Prosperity Report: It is a report released by the World Bank that provides the latest estimates and trends in global poverty and shared prosperity. So, pair 1 is not correctly matched.
● Global Hunger Index (GHI): It is jointly published by Concern Worldwide and Welthungerlife. The GHI lists countries by ‘severity’. A low score gets a country a higher ranking and implies better performance. So, pair 2 is not correctly matched.
○ Global Multidimensional Poverty Index: It is an international measure of acute multidimensional poverty covering over 100 developing countries. It complements traditional monetary poverty measures by capturing the acute deprivations in health, education, and living standards that a person faces simultaneously. It was developed by Oxford Poverty and Human Development Initiative (OPHI) with the UN Development Programme (UNDP) for inclusion in UNDP’s flagship Human Development Report.
● Global Education Monitoring Report: United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization’s (UNESCO) annual Global Education Monitoring (GEM) Report began in 2002 as the Education for All Global Monitoring Report with the aim of holding the global community to account for the commitments they made to education progress. So, pair 3 is correctly matched.
So, only one of the above pairs is correctly matched.
Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.
subject: Economics

questionText: ‘Multi Tranche Financing Facility’ is related to the provisions of lending by which one of the following?
options: c) International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
d) Asian Infrastructure and Investment Bank
explanation:
● Multi Tranche Financing Facility is a longer-term partnership between Asian Development Bank (ADB) and client country. It provides funds for complex projects which would need a longer commitment than a typical ADB investment loan could offer. It can finance multiple projects under investment programs in a sector or in various sectors.
● It has features of a standby letter of credit, and can be used to extend debt finance and advice for:
○ large stand-alone projects with interrelated components
○ investment programs with interconnected components in a sector or sectors
○ credit lines for small and medium-sized enterprises and local governments
● A multi-tranche financing facility is a type of financing arrangement in which a lender provides a borrower with a series of loans over a period of time, rather than a single loan. This type of financing can be used to fund a variety of projects or investments, such as infrastructure projects, real estate developments, or capital expenditures.
Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.
subject: Economics

questionText:
Consider the following pairs:
Sl. No. WTO Conference : Outcomes
1. Bali Package, 2013 : Peace Clause on Subsidies
2. Nairobi Package, 2015 : Trade Facilitation Agreement
3. Geneva Conference, 2022 : Agreement on Fisheries Subsidies
Which of the pairs given above is/are correct?
explanation:
● Bali Package 2013: It is the trade agreement outcome resulting from the WTO ministerial conference 2013 at Bali, Indonesia. Its two significant components are Trade Facilitation Agreement and Peace Clause on subsidies. So, pair 1 is correctly matched.
● Nairobi Package 2015: If there is a surge of cheap agro exports from 1st world to 3rd world, then 3rd world countries will have the right to temporarily increase tariffs/taxes on them, to protect their local farmers. It’s called Special Safeguard Mechanism. So, pair 2 is not correctly matched.
● Geneva Conference 2022: At the 12th Ministerial Conference held in 2022 in Geneva, WTO members forged a historic ‘Agreement on Fisheries Subsidies’, which sets new rules to curb harmful subsidies and protect global fish stocks in a manner that also recognizes the needs of fishers in developing and least-developed countries (LDCs). So, pair 3 is correctly matched.
Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.
subject: Economics

questionText: Which one of the following organisations has started the Blue Deal initiative to promote global trade and investment for developing a resilient ocean economy?
options: d) United Nations Conference on Trade and Development
explanation:
● The "Blue Deal" initiative is a United Nations Conference on Trade and Development (UNCTAD) led coalition that aims to promote global trade, investment, and innovation for creating a resilient ocean economy that benefits all. The initiative was charted at the fourth edition of the UN Oceans Forum.
● The initiative is essential for protecting the oceans' resources while boosting their economic benefits.
Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.
Relevance: The “Blue Deal” initiative was promoted at the 2022 United Nations Ocean Conference to enable the sustainable use of ocean resources for economic growth.
subject: Economics

questionText:
With reference to the United Nations (UN), consider the following statements:
1. It is the final authority to recognize either a State or a Government in the world.
2. A new State can only be admitted to the UN if it has received two-thirds of the majority votes in the UN General Assembly.
3. A State can be expelled from the organisations on the recommendations of the UN Security Council.
How many of the above statements are correct?
explanation:
● The United Nations (UN) is an international organisation founded in 1945. Currently made up of 193 Member States, the UN and its work are guided by the purposes and principles contained in its founding Charter.
● The recognition of a new State or Government is an act that only other States and Governments may grant or withhold. It generally implies readiness to assume diplomatic relations. The United Nations is neither a State nor a government, and therefore does not possess any authority to recognize either a State or a Government. As an organisation of independent States, it may admit a new State to its membership or accept the credentials of the representatives of a new Government. So, statement 1 is not correct.
● States are admitted to membership in the UN by a decision of the General Assembly upon the recommendation of the UN Security Council. If the Council recommends admission, the recommendation is presented to the General Assembly for consideration. A two-thirds majority vote is necessary in the Assembly for admission of a new State. So, statement 2 is correct.
● Article 6 of the Charter of the United Nations reads as follows: "A Member of the United Nations which has persistently violated the principles contained in the present Charter may be expelled from the Organization by the General Assembly upon the recommendation of the Security Council." So, statement 3 is correct.
● There are currently 193 Member States of the United Nations. The latest State to join the United Nations was South Sudan in 2011. Membership in the Organization, in accordance with the Charter of the United Nations, “is open to all peace-loving States that accept the obligations contained in the United Nations Charter and, in the judgement of the Organization, are able to carry out these obligations”.
So, only two of the above statements are correct.
Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.
subject: Polity

questionText: The ‘Bali Process’, sometimes mentioned in the media, aims to:
options: a) Address the challenges of people smuggling, trafficking in persons and related transnational crime
b) Provide insurance-based finance mechanism for loss and damage that occurs during climate disasters
c) Promote microfinance tools in the water and sanitation sector
d) Regulate the movements of living-modified organisms resulting from modern biotechnology from one country to another
explanation:
● Bali Process is a forum for policy dialogue, information sharing and practical cooperation to help the region address the challenges of people smuggling, trafficking in persons and related transnational crime. Since its inception in 2002, the Bali Process has effectively raised regional awareness of the consequences of people smuggling, trafficking in persons and related transnational crime.
● The Bali Process, co-chaired by Indonesia and Australia, has 49 members, including the United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR), the International Organization for Migration (IOM), the United Nations Office of Drugs and Crime (UNODC) and the International Labour Organization (ILO), as well as a number of observer countries and international agencies. It also includes the Ad Hoc Group, bringing together those most-affected member countries, and relevant international organisations, to address specific irregular migration issues in the region.
● The Regional Support Office (RSO) of the Bali Process was also established to support and strengthen practical cooperation on refugee protection and international migration, including human trafficking and smuggling and other components of migration management in the region.
Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.
subject: Polity

questionText:
Consider the following statements with reference to the Sanctions Committee of the United Nations Security Council (UNSC):
1. Its Chairperson is elected from among the non-permanent Security Council members.
2. It can impose sanction measures against State and non-State entities including asset freeze and travel ban.
3. Humanitarian supplies can only be prohibited against a State if UNSC imposes a complete ban on all forms of economic trade against it.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
explanation:
● The United Nations Security Council (UNSC) is composed of 15 Members. Five permanent members: China, France, Russian Federation, the United Kingdom, and the United States, and ten non-permanent members elected for two-year terms by the General Assembly.
● Sanctions Committees are usually created by the resolution that initially imposed the sanctions. They are created according to Article 28 of the Provisional Rules of Procedure of the Security Council and are generally referred to by the number of the UNSC resolution that imposed the sanction. Each Committee is made up of the fifteen members (5 permanent and 10 non-permanent members) of the UNSC, who elect a one-year chairperson from among the non-permanent Security Council members. The Chairperson has a secretariat and is part of the UN’s Department of Political Affairs. Decisions are taken by consensus (therefore, all the members have veto power) and in the presence of all the members (meaning that any one absence blocks the decision). So, statement 1 is correct.
● UNSC establishes Sanctions Committees to monitor the implementation and effects of embargoes or sanctions it has decided to impose against states as well as non-state entities or individuals. The Sanction committee oversees the sanctions measures imposed by the Security Council. The sanction measures include asset freeze, travel ban and arms embargo. So, statement 2 is correct.
● UNSC can impose embargoes in opposition to certain items only, such as weapons, or they can be “total”, blocking all forms of economic trade. However, even in the case of a “total embargo,” humanitarian supplies, protected by international humanitarian law under the category of goods essential for the survival of the population victim of conflict, cannot be prohibited or otherwise impeded from reaching their destination. So, statement 3 is not correct.
Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.
subject: Polity

questionText:
Consider the following pairs:
Sl. No. International Grouping : Member Countries
1. Trade and Technology Council : United States of America (USA) and European Union
2. Quad Semiconductor Supply Chain Initiative : Australia and India
3. Chip 4 Alliance : South Korea and Japan
4. World Semiconductor Council : USA and China
How many of the above pairs are correctly matched?
explanation:
● The Trade and Technology Council (TTC) is a transatlantic political body which serves as a diplomatic forum to coordinate technology and trade policy between the United States (US) and European Union (EU). The early agenda focused primarily on US-EU cooperation in technology, strategic sectors, market access, trade, democratic values and rule of law in the digital world, supply chain resilience, and the global trade order. India is not a member of this organisation. So, pair 1 is correctly matched.
● The Quad Semiconductor Supply Chain Initiative is a partnership between the governments of the United States, Japan, Australia, and India to promote the development of a resilient and secure semiconductor supply chain in the Indo-Pacific region. So, pair 2 is correctly matched.
● In 2021, the US proposed the formation of the Chip 4 Alliance, a strategic alliance that includes South Korea, Japan, and Taiwan. Overall, the intention of the alliance is to restructure the global supply chain such that it is less reliant on China, with a particular focus on the following goals: Geographically diversify manufacturing capacity away from China; Protect the intellectual property of companies from member nations; Coordinate uniform export controls with regard to China; Encourage favourable distribution terms among friendly nations. India is not a member of this group. So, pair 3 is correctly matched.
● The World Semiconductor Council (WSC) is an international forum that brings together industry leaders to address issues of global concern to the semiconductor industry. It is comprised of the Semiconductor Industry Associations (SIAs) of the United States, Korea, Japan, Europe, China and Chinese Taipei. Indian semiconductor industry associations are not members of this organisation. So, pair 4 is correctly matched.
So, all four of the above pairs are correctly matched.
Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.
Relevance: There is an increasing concern about the security of the semiconductor supply chain across the world.
subject: Economics

questionText: The Convention on Supplementary Compensation is related to which one of the following?
options: a) Compensation for damages caused by nuclear accident
c) Land acquisition for infrastructural development
d) Compensation in case of railway accidents
explanation:
● The Convention on Supplementary Compensation (CSC) aims at establishing a minimum national compensation amount and at further increasing the amount of compensation through public funds to be made available by the Contracting Parties should the national amount be insufficient to compensate for the damage caused by a nuclear incident.
● The Convention is open not only to States that are party to either the Vienna Convention on Civil Liability for Nuclear Damage or the Paris Convention on Third Party Liability in the Field of Nuclear Energy (including any amendments to either), but also to other States provided that their national legislation is consistent with uniform rules on civil liability laid down in the Annex to the Convention.
Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.
subject: Polity

questionText: Which one of the following statements best describes the terms “Torkham, Ghulam Khan and Chaman”, recently seen in the news?
options: a) Hydropower projects in Afghanistan being constructed with Indian assistance.
b) Designated terrorists under the UN Security Council sanction list.
c) Strategic border crossings points between Pakistan and Afghanistan.
d) Recent discoveries of ancient archaeological sites in Afghanistan.
explanation:
● Chaman is a city and the headquarters of the Chaman District in Balochistan, Pakistan. It is located near the Afghanistan-Pakistan border. This district was carved out of the Qila Abdullah District. The city is situated south of the Wesh–Chaman border crossing with the neighbouring Kandahar province of Afghanistan. Is the Chaman border connected to Pakistan. Wesh–Chaman border crossing. The Wesh–Chaman border crossing is one of the major international border crossings between Afghanistan and Pakistan. Located on the Pak-Afghan border, it leads north from the town of Chaman, Chaman District, Balochistan into Wesh, Afghanistan in Spin Boldak District, Kandahar province.
● Torkham is a Pakistani town in the Khyber District, located at the end of the N-5 National Highway. It is also the location of the Torkham Border Crossing with Afghanistan. The Torkham border crossing is a major border crossing between the two countries, connecting Nangarhar province of Afghanistan with Pakistan's Khyber Pakhtunkhwa province. It is the busiest port of entry between the two countries, serving as a major transporting, shipping, and receiving site.
● Ghulam Khan is on the border between Pakistan and Afghanistan. It is the third most important border crossing point between Pakistan and Afghanistan, after Chaman and Torkham.
● Therefore, option c is the correct answer.

questionText:
Consider the following statements with respect to Partnership for Global Infrastructure Investment (PGII):
1. It is a collaborative effort to finance infrastructure projects in developing countries through public and private investments.
2. It was officially launched during the G20 summit in Germany in 2022.
3. It is seen as a response to China’s Belt and Road Initiative (BRI).
How many of the statement(s) given above is/are correct?
explanation:
● The infrastructure plan was first announced in June 2021 during the G7 (or Group of Seven) summit in the UK. The G7 countries include the United Kingdom, the United States, Canada, France, Germany, Italy, Japan, and the European Union (EU). US President Joe Biden had called it the Build Back Better World (B3W) framework. However, it did not register much progress. It is a collaborative effort by G7 nations to fund infrastructure projects in developing nations. So, statement 1 is correct.
● In 2022, during the G7 summit in Germany, the PGII was officially launched as a joint initiative to help fund infrastructure projects in developing countries through public and private investments. So, statement 2 is incorrect.
● Global Infrastructure Investment (PGII) — a West-led initiative for funding infrastructure projects across the world, seen as a counter to China’s Belt and Road Initiative (BRI). So, statement 3 is correct.
● The U.S. along with its allies had announced the launch of the Build Back Better World (B3W) in 2021 with the aim of narrowing the 40 trillion dollar infrastructure gap in the developing world.
● Partnership for Global Infrastructure and Investment is therefore a relaunch of the B3W plan.
● The PGII is being seen as the G7’s counter to China’s multi-trillion dollar Belt and Road Initiative (BRI) to build connectivity, infrastructure, and trade projects in Asia, Europe, Africa, and Latin America.
● Therefore, option b is the correct answer.

questionText:
Consider the following:
1.Paracels
2.Spratlys
3.Natuna
What is the correct order of the above islands in the South China Sea from north to south?
explanation:
Hence, the correct order is 1,2,3.
Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.

questionText:
Consider the following statements with respect to Nagorno-Karabakh Region:
1. It is a mountainous region lying north of the Caucasus mountains.
2. It is geographically a part of Azerbaijan.
3. The majority of its population is ethnic Armenian.
Which of the statement(s)given above is/are correct?
explanation:
● The Nagorno-Karabakh region is a mountainous and forested area in Azerbaijan that is part of the Lesser Caucasus Mountain range. It's also known as Artsakh, a name primarily used by Armenians.
● Nagorno-Karabakh is a mountainous and heavily forested region lying south of the Caucasus mountains. The region includes the north-eastern flank of the Karabakh Range of the Lesser Caucasus and extends from the crest line of the range to the margin of the Kura River lowland at its foot. So, statement 1 is incorrect.
● The region is internationally recognized as part of Azerbaijan, but the majority of its population is ethnic Armenian and rejects Azerbaijani rule. The region has been a source of tension since 1917, when both Azerbaijan and Armenia claimed it after the fall of the Russian Empire. So, statements 2 and 3 are correct.
● Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.

questionText:
Consider the following statements with respect to Haiti:
1. It is situated between Cuba and Puerto Rico.
2. The Atlantic Ocean borders Haiti’s Southern shores.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
explanation:
● Haiti occupies the western third of the island of Hispaniola, situated between Cuba and Puerto Rico, which it shares with the Dominican Republic. So, statement 1 is correct.
● The Atlantic Ocean borders Haiti's northern shores, while the Caribbean Sea is to the west and south. So, statement 2 is incorrect.
● Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.

questionText:
Consider the following statements with respect to NATO
1. NATO is a security alliance of more than 30 countries from North America and Europe only.
2. Maximum funding to NATO is done by United States of America
3. Its headquarter is located in Vienna.
Which of the statement(s) given above is/are incorrect?
explanation:
● NATO was created by 12 countries from Europe and North America on 4 April 1949.
● Since then, 19 more countries have joined NATO through nine rounds of enlargement (in 1952, 1955, 1982, 1999, 2004, 2009, 2017, 2020 and 2023). Article 10 of the North Atlantic Treaty sets out how countries can join the Alliance. It states that membership is open to any "European State in a position to further the principles of this Treaty and to contribute to the security of the North Atlantic area".
● Any decision to invite a country to join the Alliance is taken by the North Atlantic Council, NATO's principal political decision-making body, on the basis of consensus among all Allies.
● At present, NATO has 31 member countries. These countries, called NATO Allies, are sovereign states that come together through NATO to discuss political and security issues and make collective decisions by consensus.
Turkey is located in Asia. Hence, Statement 1 is incorrect.
● In 2006, NATO Defence Ministers agreed to commit a minimum of 2% of their Gross Domestic Product (GDP) to defence spending to continue to ensure the Alliance's military readiness. The combined wealth of the non-US Allies, measured in GDP, is almost equal to that of the United States. However, non-US Allies together spend less than half of what the United States spends on defence. The volume of US defence expenditure represents approximately two thirds of the defence spending of the Alliance as a whole. Hence, Statement 2 is correct.
● NATO Headquarters is the political and administrative centre of the Alliance. It is located at Boulevard Leopold III in Brussels, Belgium. Hence, Statement 3 is incorrect.
Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.

questionText:
Consider the following statements with respect to 2+2 Talks:
1. As per the agreement, Foreign and Home Ministers meet in a ‘2+2’ format to discuss strategic and security issues.
2. India has a 2+2 format of dialogue with all the Quad countries.
Which of the statement(s) given above is/are incorrect?
explanation:
● The 2+2 dialogue is a format of meeting of the foreign and defence ministers of India and its allies on strategic and security issues. It enables the partners to better understand and appreciate each other’s strategic concerns and sensitivities. Hence, Statement 1 is incorrect.
● India has 2+2 dialogues with five key strategic partners: the US, Australia, Japan, Russia and the UK. Besides Russia, other three countries are also India’s partners in the Quad. The inaugural 2+2 dialogue with Australia was held in September 2021 in New Delhi. Hence, Statement 2 is correct.
● Therefore, Option (a) is the correct answer.

questionText:
Consider the following pairs:
S No. Places : Countries
1. Pasamlung : Nepal
2. Erez : Egypt
3. Haldibari : Bangladesh
Which of the above pairs are correctly matched?
explanation:
● Pasamlung: Since 1984, talks between Bhutan and China have largely focused on two separate areas of dispute, including Doklam and other areas in Bhutan's West, near the India-China-Bhutan trijunction, and the Jakarlung and Pasamlung valleys located near Tibet to Bhutan's North. Hence, Pair 1 is incorrectly matched.
● Erez: Among sites that Hamas overran in that attack was the Erez border crossing between Gaza and Israel. Hence, pair 2 is incorrectly matched.
● Haldibari: The two sides agreed on a final location survey for the propose cross-border rail link connecting Kokrajhar in Assam to Gelephu in Bhutan. The two sides also agreed to consider establishing a rail link between Banarhat in West Bengal and Samtse in Bhutan.
○ It was also decided to designate Haldibari (West Bengal) to Chilahati (Bangladesh) rail. Hence, Pair 3 is incorrectly matched.
Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.

questionText:
Consider the following statements with respect to USS Dwight D. Eisenhower:
1. USS Dwight D. Eisenhower is a nuclear-powered aircraft carrier.
2. It is the first ship named after the President of France.
Which of the statement(s) given above is/are correct?
explanation:
● The nuclear-powered USS Dwight D. Eisenhower Carrier Strike Group (CSG) has been directed to move to the Eastern Mediterranean - a place right next to the ongoing conflict between Israel and the militant organisation Hamas. Hence, Statement 1 is correct.
● Commissioned in 1977, the ship is the second of the ten Nimitz-class aircraft carriers currently in service, and is the first ship named after the 34th President of the United States and General of the Army Dwight D. Eisenhower. Hence, Statement 2 is incorrect.
Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.

questionText: ‘Business Ready Project’ often seen in news is launched by:
options: a) Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development
c) United Nations Conference on Trade and Development
explanation:
● The World Bank Group launched the Business Ready (BREADY) Project. Business Ready reflects a more balanced approach toward evaluating a country’s business and investment climate and to make it ready for a dynamic private sector.
● Business Ready improves upon and replaces the World Bank Group’s earlier Doing Business project. The World Bank had scrapped the Doing Business report (in 2021) after a probe revealed that rankings for China, Saudi Arabia, UAE and Azerbaijan had been altered.
● It will focus on ten topics covering a firm’s life cycle –business entry, business location, utility services, labour, financial services, international trade, taxation, dispute resolution, market competition, and business insolvency.
Therefore, Option (b) is the correct answer.
